# MD2PDF

> Convert Markdown documents to beautifully styled PDFs with multiple themes. Free, open-source, runs entirely in the browser.

MD2PDF is a free web tool that converts `.md` (Markdown) files into clean, styled PDFs and HTML files. Choose from 11 visual styles and customize with your own CSS.

## Features

- Live split-pane editor with real-time preview
- GitHub-flavored Markdown (GFM) support
- Syntax highlighting for 180+ programming languages
- 11 visual styles: Notion, GitHub, Minimal, Academic, Corporate, LaTeX, Dracula, Newspaper, Handwritten, Terminal, Pastel
- Custom CSS injection for full control over styling
- One-click PDF export (continuous single-page)
- HTML export with all styles inlined
- Dark and light theme toggle
- Drag and drop file upload
- 5 document templates: CV/Resume, Report, Documentation, Changelog, Meeting Notes
- Word count and reading time indicator
- Split view or preview-only mode
- Auto-save drafts to localStorage
- Resizable editor/preview panes
- Keyboard shortcuts
- Runs entirely in the browser ŌĆö no server, no data sent anywhere, no signup

## Available Styles

| Style | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Notion | Clean, modern sans-serif (default) |
| GitHub | Classic GitHub markdown rendering |
| Minimal | Elegant serif, no decorations |
| Academic | Formal justified text, scholarly feel |
| Corporate | Professional with blue accents and styled table headers |
| LaTeX | Simulates academic papers with serif typography |
| Dracula | Vibrant purple/pink/green color palette |
| Newspaper | Editorial typography with Playfair Display |
| Handwritten | Cursive font with notebook-lined background |
| Terminal | Green-on-black monospace, hacker aesthetic |
| Pastel | Soft pink/purple/teal with rounded elements |

## Usage

1. Visit https://md2pdf.studio
2. Write or paste Markdown in the editor, or drag and drop a `.md` file
3. Choose a style from the dropdown in the preview panel
4. Optionally add custom CSS for further customization
5. Click "Export" to download as PDF or HTML

## AI Agent Integration (Skill)

MD2PDF provides an installable **Skill** for AI agents (Claude, Custom GPTs, Gemini, etc.) in the Claude Skills format. Agents can create, update, and share styled documents programmatically.

Install the skill by copying or downloading `SKILL.md` from the "AI Skill" section in the app, or use the REST API directly:

- **Create document:** `POST https://md2pdf.studio/api/save` with the markdown as body (`Content-Type: text/plain`). Returns `{ id, editKey, url }`.
- **Update document:** `PUT https://md2pdf.studio/api/update/{id}` with the updated markdown, requires `X-Edit-Key` header.
- **Response:** shareable URL that displays the document with the chosen style.
- **Limits:** 500 KB max, 10 req/min per IP, links expire 90 days after last update.
- **No authentication required.**

The skill is intentionally written in English as it is the technical standard for AI agent instructions.

## Templates

Pre-built document templates available:
- CV / Resume
- Monthly Report
- Project Documentation
- Changelog
- Meeting Notes

## Keyboard Shortcuts

- `Ctrl+S` / `Cmd+S` ŌĆö Export to PDF
- `Ctrl+Shift+L` / `Cmd+Shift+L` ŌĆö Toggle dark/light theme
- `Tab` ŌĆö Insert tab in editor

## Supported Markdown

- Headings, paragraphs, bold, italic, strikethrough
- Ordered and unordered lists, task lists
- Code blocks with syntax highlighting (JavaScript, Python, Go, Rust, SQL, etc.)
- Tables
- Blockquotes
- Images
- Horizontal rules
- Links

## Tech Stack

- Pure HTML, CSS, JavaScript (no framework, no build step)
- marked.js for Markdown parsing
- highlight.js for syntax highlighting
- GitHub Markdown CSS as base styling
- Browser native print-to-PDF for export
